ai_agent.role: Manages material quantity estimation from architectural plans using BlueBeam, Software Globin, and ChatGPT. Produces detailed takeoff lists that feed Budget and Purchasing departments. Handles spec home templates and custom home unique takeoffs.
workflow_chains: project_executivo_received -> bluebeam_calibration -> material_quantification -> waste_factor_10pct -> compile_word_list -> save_to_drive -> notify_budget
vendor_takeoff_enabled -> send_plans_to_vendor -> receive_vendor_quote -> cross_reference_internal -> validate_variance -> approve_or_escalate
model_change -> identify_affected_categories -> re_quantify -> move_old_to_revision -> replace_with_new -> notify_budget_and_purchasing
block_takeoff -> globin_analysis -> chatgpt_estimation -> calculate_components -> add_waste_factor -> send_to_budget

core_knowledge.description: BlueBeam scale calibration using reference dimensions (cotas) is MANDATORY before any measurement. If calibration uses wrong reference dimension, ALL measurements are proportionally wrong and must be re-done. Calibration error is the most common source of takeoff inaccuracy.

A standard 10% waste factor is added to every material quantity in the takeoff. This accounts for cutting waste, breakage, installation variance, and material defects. Different subs may use slightly different quantities (~10% natural variance is considered acceptable).

Software Globin auto-detects certain quantities from construction PDFs (areas, lengths, counts). Gabriela uses ChatGPT as a calculation assistant for unit conversions and complex volume calculations. Results are cross-verified between Globin, BlueBeam, and manual calculations. Never blindly trust software output.

Complete takeoff covers: concrete (slabs, footers/vergas), CMU/lintel blocks, drywall, flooring/revestimentos, countertops, lighting (pontual), plumbing fixtures, doors and windows (with opening direction), exterior finish (stucco, painting), roofing (with angle calculation for shingle area), trims, brackets (mao francesa). Each category has specific measurement methodology.

Spec homes: takeoff done once per model, rarely revised, template quantities reused across all houses of that model. Custom homes: every project requires unique takeoff from scratch. Client changes during construction (tile instead of vinyl, different countertop) require partial re-takeoff of affected categories only.

Some vendors do their own takeoff from plans (foundation materials, precast, trusses). Items are marked 'Enable Vendor Takeoff' in the system. Vendor receives plans, returns total price. Internal team cross-references vendor quantities against internal estimates for reasonableness. Under 10% variance accepted; 10-15% flagged for Budget review; over 15% escalated to Samuel Torres.

CAS Creed and CMEX use completely different item naming systems for the same materials. Example: CAS Creed '8x8x16 regular block' = CMEX 'Standard CMU Block 8-inch'. No permanent equivalence table exists. Manual item-by-item mapping required for every price comparison. 15-30% price difference possible between vendors for same material.

Block takeoff includes: regular blocks, lintel blocks, rough blocks, 3/4 blocks, column blocks (blocos de coluna), sand (areia), cement (cimento), freight (frete), environmental fee (taxa ambiental). Each component calculated separately using Globin + ChatGPT estimation.

daily_workflows.name: BlueBeam Material Quantification
steps: Receive project executivo PDF from Luana (via engineer)
Open PDF in BlueBeam
Calibrate scale using reference dimensions (cotas) - MANDATORY
Trace areas for concrete slabs, footers, and lintels
Count and measure CMU/lintel blocks
Measure drywall wall areas
Calculate flooring and countertop areas
Count lighting points, plumbing fixtures
Count and classify doors and windows (with opening direction)
Calculate exterior finish areas (stucco, painting)
Calculate roofing area with angle adjustment for shingles
Measure trim lengths and bracket quantities
Compile all results manually into Word document
Add 10% waste factor to each item
Save takeoff PDF to Google Drive in project folder
daily_workflows.frequency: Per new model (spec) or per project (custom)
critical_decisions: Whether to include non-standard items for custom homes
Waste factor adjustment for irregular shapes

daily_workflows.name: Block and Concrete Estimation (No Vendor Takeoff)
steps: Import construction plans into Software Globin
Run Globin auto-detection for areas and counts
Use ChatGPT for volume calculations and unit conversions
Cross-verify Globin results with BlueBeam measurements
Calculate all block components: regular, lintel, rough, 3/4, column
Calculate ancillary materials: sand, cement, freight, environmental fee
Forward quantities to Budget team for cost calculation (quantity x unit price)
critical_decisions: Unit conversion accuracy (sqft to sqm, cubic yards to cubic feet)

daily_workflows.name: Vendor Takeoff Management
steps: Identify services where vendor does their own takeoff (foundation, precast, trusses)
Mark item as 'Enable Vendor Takeoff' in takeoff list
Send project plans to vendor via email
Receive vendor's price/quantity response
Cross-reference vendor quantities against internal BlueBeam estimates
Evaluate variance: <10% accept, 10-15% flag for Budget, >15% escalate to Samuel Torres
Record final approved quantities
critical_decisions: Whether vendor methodology is superior to internal measurement

daily_workflows.name: Document Version Management
steps: Upload new takeoff documents to project folder in Google Drive
Move old version to 'revision' subfolder
Replace active document with new version
Manually notify Budget team of updated quantities (no automated notification)
critical_decisions: Whether change affects only specific categories or entire takeoff
Whether Budget team needs to revise their estimates

features_used: Take-off page (/quotation/take_off) - define service quantities per model
Model 1774 reference: 19 cost codes, 33 services, 74 components
Supply type classification: Stocked (27 items), Quoted (6 items), By Sub (10 items)
Enable Vendor Takeoff - transfer takeoff responsibility to vendor
Plans tracking: 17/16 plans completed (IN PROGRESS status)

handoffs.trigger: Floor Plan Complete - project executivo PDF received from engineer via Luana
handoffs.data_exchanged: Project executivo PDF, floor plan, design book, spec sheet, model type (spec/custom), county
handoffs.impact: Takeoff cannot begin without calibrated project executivo PDF. Floor plan quality directly affects takeoff accuracy.

handoffs.trigger: Takeoff complete - material quantities compiled and saved to Drive
handoffs.data_exchanged: Takeoff PDF with all material quantities by category, waste factor included, supply type classification
handoffs.impact: Budget team cannot create cost estimates without takeoff quantities. Currently NO automatic notification when takeoff is updated.

handoffs.trigger: Takeoff quantities approved and budget created
handoffs.data_exchanged: Material quantities list for procurement, vendor specifications
handoffs.impact: Purchasing depends on takeoff accuracy for correct order quantities

handoffs.trigger: Material quantities available from blocks phase onward
handoffs.data_exchanged: Material quantities and specifications for scheduling material delivery timing
handoffs.impact: Scheduling uses takeoff quantities to coordinate material delivery with construction phases

handoffs.trigger: Floor plan and design documents ready
handoffs.data_exchanged: Floor plan documents for permit application submission
handoffs.impact: Permit application requires floor plan documents from the design/takeoff team

pain_points.description: Company CPUs are too weak to run architecture and takeoff software (BlueBeam, AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape). Candida and Gabriela must use personal computers.
pain_points.current_workaround: Staff use personal computers with adequate GPU; company reimburses or provides allowance
pain_points.proposed_solution: Procure workstation-class laptops with dedicated GPU for all architecture/takeoff staff

pain_points.description: Manual BlueBeam-to-Word compilation. All measurement results from BlueBeam must be manually compiled into a Word document. This is slow, error-prone, and the primary bottleneck in the takeoff process.
pain_points.current_workaround: Gabriela and Candida manually transfer all measurements to Word one by one
pain_points.proposed_solution: Use BlueBeam's paid spreadsheet integration feature (already licensed but never used) or build automated export pipeline from BlueBeam to structured data format

pain_points.description: BlueBeam auto-spreadsheet feature never used. The paid feature that links BlueBeam measurements directly to spreadsheets has never been explored or configured.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual compilation continues; feature sits unused
pain_points.proposed_solution: Training session on BlueBeam spreadsheet integration; pilot with one model to validate workflow improvement

pain_points.description: Takeoff changes do not automatically notify Budget team. When quantities are updated, Budget continues working with outdated data until manually informed.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual notification via email or verbal communication (often delayed or forgotten)
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated notification system that alerts Budget team when any takeoff document is updated, with change summary showing affected categories

pain_points.description: 3,500 items to update manually when models change. When a model design changes, there is no template/duplication mechanism to propagate changes across related takeoffs.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual item-by-item update of all 3,500+ items across all affected takeoffs
pain_points.proposed_solution: Template-based takeoff system with model inheritance: change a parent model and propagate to all derived takeoffs with delta review

pain_points.description: Vendor naming conventions incompatible between CAS Creed and CMEX. No equivalence table exists; manual mapping required for every price comparison.
pain_points.current_workaround: Team manually searches CMEX catalog item-by-item to find CAS Creed equivalents
pain_points.proposed_solution: Build and maintain a vendor nomenclature equivalence database with fuzzy matching support

pain_points.description: Custom home takeoff volatility. Clients change specifications during construction (tile instead of vinyl, different countertop materials) requiring partial re-takeoff of affected categories.
pain_points.current_workaround: Re-quantify only affected categories; old takeoff goes to revision folder
pain_points.proposed_solution: Modular takeoff system where individual categories can be updated independently with automatic downstream notification

tribal_knowledge.description: Gabriela has developed refined waste estimation knowledge beyond the standard 10% factor. For irregular shapes, complex floor plans, and specific materials (e.g., tile with pattern matching), she adjusts waste factors upward. For large uniform areas, she may reduce slightly. This nuanced estimation prevents both material shortages and excessive overordering.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - replacement would revert to flat 10% which causes either shortages (complex shapes) or excess inventory (simple shapes)

tribal_knowledge.description: Different subcontractors mount blocks differently, leading to approximately 10% natural variance in block counts for the same house model. This is considered acceptable by the company. Knowledge of which subs consistently over-request vs under-request helps prevent disputes and material waste.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - could lead to unnecessary disputes with subs over quantity differences

tribal_knowledge.description: Working knowledge of how CAS Creed item names map to CMEX, Cemex, and other vendor catalogs. No formal equivalence table exists. Includes knowledge of unit differences (per unit vs per pallet vs per cubic yard) that must be normalized before price comparison.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - vendor price comparison would become impossible without this knowledge; risk of overpaying for materials

tribal_knowledge.description: Knowledge of which reference dimensions to use for calibration per architect and engineer (some architects use different scaling conventions). Includes awareness of common calibration pitfalls and how to detect when calibration is off by checking known dimensions.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - incorrect calibration leads to proportionally wrong measurements across entire takeoff

topics: BlueBeam interface and navigation
Mandatory scale calibration using reference dimensions
Area measurement for floors, walls, and roofing
Linear measurement for trim, baseboard, and casings
Point counting for fixtures, lights, and hardware
Volume calculation for concrete (slabs, footers, lintels)
10% waste factor application
Compiling results into Word document format

topics: Software Globin workflow: import, auto-detect, manual review
ChatGPT for calculation assistance and unit conversions
Cross-verification between Globin, BlueBeam, and manual calculations
Block component calculation: regular, lintel, rough, 3/4, column
Never trust software output blindly - verification protocol
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Globin import process, ChatGPT prompt best practices, verification methodology

topics: Enable Vendor Takeoff feature and when to use it
Cross-referencing vendor quantities against internal estimates
Variance thresholds: <10% accept, 10-15% flag, >15% escalate
CAS Creed vs CMEX nomenclature mapping
Unit normalization for price comparison (each vs pallet vs cubic yard)
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Vendor takeoff process, variance escalation, nomenclature challenges

topics: Foundation materials: ordered after UP Inspection, arrive AFTER approval
Trusses: ETA = Foundation Material ETA + 2 weeks; never Monday delivery
Blocks: arrive 2 days AFTER slab pouring (cure time)
Lumber: delivered to back of property (theft risk)
Trim set: requires Garage Door + Electronic Locker first
Tile and vinyl: must arrive before labor is scheduled
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Material delivery timing rules, CPM phase dependencies

compliance_requirements.requirement: BlueBeam calibration must be verified before any measurement is recorded
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal quality standard - measurement accuracy requirement
compliance_requirements.verification: Calibration reference dimension documented in takeoff file header

compliance_requirements.requirement: Foundation concrete quantities must match exactly between internal takeoff and vendor takeoff (zero tolerance)
compliance_requirements.verification: Both internal and vendor quantities documented and compared; any discrepancy requires engineering review

compliance_requirements.requirement: All takeoff documents must be versioned with old versions moved to revision folder
compliance_requirements.verification: Revision folder contains superseded versions; active folder contains only current version

compliance_requirements.requirement: 10% waste factor applied to all material quantities
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal procurement standard to prevent material shortages
compliance_requirements.verification: Waste factor line item visible in compiled takeoff document for each material category

key_metrics.target: < 5 business days for spec home; < 10 for custom
key_metrics.measurement: Days from project executivo receipt to takeoff PDF saved in Drive

key_metrics.target: < 10% variance between takeoff and actual material used
key_metrics.measurement: Post-construction comparison of takeoff quantities vs actual PO quantities
Percentage of vendor takeoffs exceeding 15% variance from internal estimate

key_metrics.target: < 1 business day from takeoff update to Budget team notification
key_metrics.measurement: Time between takeoff document update and Budget team acknowledgement

key_metrics.target: All 19 cost codes covered per model takeoff
key_metrics.measurement: Number of cost codes with complete material quantities vs total required
